requirement: the format specification is well-structured and readable

findings.observation: Order moves concept-before-detail: Conformance and Key Terms precede the file format, the format precedes the evaluation phases, and each construct (Model/Target/Factor/Requirement) is introduced before its dependent rules.
Schema code blocks with aligned requiredness comments and a recommended-body-sections table make structure scannable; the body example and Appendix A give minimal, realistic, copy-and-adapt examples.
The Analyze and Requirement sections are dense, intricate prose that is harder to scan than the rest, keeping readability short of outstanding.

rationale: Logical ordering, scannable schema blocks/tables, plain prose, and realistic examples satisfy the well-structured-and-readable criterion. A few dense passages keep it at target rather than outstanding.
